_note: Structured remediation KB for NSS, keyed by check_id family prefix (exact check_id entries may be added and take precedence via RemediationKB.lookup). CLI is generic Cisco IOS/IOS-XE unless noted; NX-OS/ASA differ where flagged. detail_for() falls back to a finding's own inline remediation for anything unmapped.

MGMT.risk: Weak or unencrypted device management (missing enable secret, Type-7/cleartext passwords, no AAA, Telnet or SSHv1, unrestricted VTY, HTTP management) lets an attacker reach the control plane, brute-force or sniff admin credentials, and take administrative control of the device.
steps: Set an encrypted enable secret and encrypt all plaintext passwords.
Enable AAA and require authenticated, authorized, accounted login.
Force SSHv2 and remove Telnet/HTTP; restrict VTY with an access-class ACL.
Apply an exec-timeout on all lines and disable the AUX port.
MGMT.gui: N/A (CLI-managed). On DNA Center / device manager, apply the corresponding device-hardening template.
MGMT.cli:
enable secret <STRONG-SECRET>
service password-encryption
aaa new-model
aaa authentication login default group tacacs+ local
ip ssh version 2
line vty 0 4
 transport input ssh
 access-class MGMT-ACL in
 exec-timeout 5 0
no ip http server
ip http secure-server
line aux 0
 no exec
 transport input none
MGMT.verify: show run | include enable secret|service password-encryption|aaa new-model|ip ssh version ; show run | section ^line vty ; show ip ssh
MGMT.rollback: Reverse each change with its 'no' form (e.g. 'no ip ssh version 2', 'no aaa new-model'). Note: removing AAA can lock you out — keep a console session open.
MGMT.impact: Non-disruptive to data-plane traffic. Administrators must switch to SSH; verify the MGMT-ACL permits the admin subnets BEFORE applying access-class to avoid lockout.
references: Cisco Guide to Harden Cisco IOS Devices

CRYPTO.risk: Weak cryptographic posture (SSHv1, small RSA keys, weak DH groups, cleartext/Type-7 secrets, SNMP v1/v2c) allows management sessions and community strings to be intercepted or brute-forced.
steps: Generate a >=2048-bit RSA key and force SSH version 2.
Replace SNMP v1/v2c with SNMPv3 authPriv.
Use 'enable secret' (Type-8/9 scrypt/pbkdf2 where supported) rather than Type-7.
CRYPTO.cli:
crypto key generate rsa modulus 2048
ip ssh version 2
ip ssh server algorithm encryption aes256-ctr aes192-ctr
snmp-server group NSSGRP v3 priv
snmp-server user NSSUSER NSSGRP v3 auth sha <AUTHPW> priv aes 256 <PRIVPW>
CRYPTO.verify: show ip ssh ; show crypto key mypubkey rsa ; show snmp user
CRYPTO.rollback: Regenerate keys / remove SNMPv3 config with 'no' forms as required.
CRYPTO.impact: Regenerating the RSA key drops active SSH sessions briefly. Non-disruptive to data plane.

CTRL.risk: An unprotected control plane (no CoPP, unauthenticated routing adjacencies, unauthenticated NTP, no BPDU Guard, CDP/LLDP leaking topology) lets an attacker DoS the CPU, inject false routes, or map the network.
steps: Apply Control Plane Policing (CoPP) to rate-limit control traffic.
Authenticate routing protocols (OSPF/EIGRP MD5/SHA, BGP password).
Authenticate NTP and enable BPDU Guard on access edge; disable CDP/LLDP on untrusted ports.
CTRL.cli:
! OSPF authentication
router ospf 1
 area 0 authentication message-digest
interface <IF>
 ip ospf message-digest-key 1 md5 <KEY>
! BGP
router bgp <ASN>
 neighbor <PEER> password <KEY>
! NTP + edge
ntp authenticate
ntp authentication-key 1 md5 <KEY>
ntp server <IP> key 1
spanning-tree portfast bpduguard default
no cdp run
CTRL.verify: show policy-map control-plane ; show ip ospf interface brief ; show ntp status ; show spanning-tree summary
CTRL.rollback: Remove authentication keys / CoPP policy with 'no' forms. Coordinate routing-auth changes with peers to avoid adjacency loss.
CTRL.impact: Routing-auth changes must be applied to BOTH peers or the adjacency drops. CoPP mis-scoped policies can drop legitimate control traffic — test in a window.

DATA.risk: Weak data-plane hardening (no uRPF/anti-spoofing, IP source routing, ICMP redirects, missing edge ACLs) enables source-address spoofing, traffic redirection, and reconnaissance.
steps: Enable unicast RPF on external interfaces.
Disable IP source routing and ICMP redirects/unreachables on edge interfaces.
Apply anti-spoofing ingress ACLs at the perimeter.
DATA.cli:
no ip source-route
interface <EXT-IF>
 ip verify unicast source reachable-via rx
 no ip redirects
 no ip proxy-arp
DATA.verify: show run | include ip source-route ; show ip interface <EXT-IF> | include verify|redirect|proxy
DATA.rollback: Re-enable with 'no ip verify ...' / 'ip redirects' as needed.
DATA.impact: uRPF strict mode can drop asymmetrically-routed traffic — use 'reachable-via any' where asymmetric routing is expected.
references: Cisco Guide to Harden Cisco IOS Devices

SVC.risk: Unnecessary services (small-servers, finger, BOOTP, PAD, MOP, unused HTTP/DHCP) expand the attack surface and have historically carried remote-exploitable bugs.
steps: Disable all unused management/discovery services.
Disable TCP/UDP small servers and legacy protocols.
SVC.cli:
no service tcp-small-servers
no service udp-small-servers
no ip finger
no service finger
no ip bootp server
no service pad
no mop enabled
SVC.rollback: Re-enable a specific service only if a documented dependency requires it.
SVC.impact: Non-disruptive on modern networks; confirm no legacy device depends on BOOTP/MOP before disabling.
references: Cisco Guide to Harden Cisco IOS Devices

SW.risk: Weak L2 hardening (DTP auto-trunking, default native VLAN, no port-security, no DHCP snooping / Dynamic ARP Inspection) enables VLAN hopping, MAC flooding, rogue DHCP, and ARP spoofing.
steps: Set access ports to mode access and disable DTP negotiation.
Enable port-security on access ports; move the native VLAN off VLAN 1.
Enable DHCP snooping and Dynamic ARP Inspection on user VLANs.
SW.cli:
interface range <ACCESS-PORTS>
 switchport mode access
 switchport nonegotiate
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 2
 switchport port-security violation restrict
ip dhcp snooping
ip dhcp snooping vlan <USER-VLANS>
ip arp inspection vlan <USER-VLANS>
SW.verify: show interfaces status ; show port-security ; show ip dhcp snooping ; show ip arp inspection
SW.rollback: Remove with 'no switchport port-security' / 'no ip dhcp snooping' as needed.
SW.impact: port-security violation shutdown can err-disable a port on a MAC change; 'restrict' is safer. Configure DHCP-snooping trust on uplinks/server ports first or DHCP breaks.

WIFI.risk: Weak wireless security (WEP/WPA-TKIP, PSK on enterprise SSIDs, no 802.11w management-frame protection, no rogue/WIDS) lets an attacker crack keys, deauth clients, or stand up an evil-twin AP.
steps: Use WPA2/WPA3-Enterprise (802.1X) and remove WEP/TKIP/PSK on corporate SSIDs.
Enable Protected Management Frames (802.11w).
Enable rogue-AP detection / aWIPS.
WIFI.gui: Catalyst 9800 WebUI: Configuration > Tags & Profiles > WLANs > Security.
WIFI.cli:
wlan CORP 1 CORP
 security wpa wpa3
 security wpa akm dot1x
 security pmf mandatory
 no security wpa wpa2
 no shutdown
wireless wps rogue detection
WIFI.verify: show wlan summary ; show wlan id 1 ; show wireless wps summary
WIFI.rollback: Restore the prior security profile; re-enabling WPA2 personal is discouraged.
WIFI.impact: Changing SSID security re-authenticates all clients on that WLAN; schedule a window. 802.11w mandatory can drop legacy clients.

NGFW.risk: Weak firewall policy (any-any permit rules, no threat inspection, no logging on ACEs, unrestricted management/VPN) allows unfiltered lateral movement and hides malicious traffic.
steps: Remove permit ip any any rules; scope source/destination/service to least privilege.
Enable IPS/malware/URL inspection and log on deny (and key permit) rules.
Restrict device management and VPN to trusted sources.
NGFW.gui: FMC: Policies > Access Control; ASDM: Configuration > Firewall > Access Rules.
NGFW.cli:
! ASA — replace the any-any rule with a scoped one
no access-list OUTSIDE_IN extended permit ip any any
access-list OUTSIDE_IN extended permit tcp <SRC> host <DST> eq <PORT> log
access-group OUTSIDE_IN in interface outside
! FTD/Firepower — set inspection + logging in the Access Control Policy (FMC)
NGFW.verify: show access-list ; show run access-group ; (FMC) review the Access Control Policy rule table
NGFW.rollback: Restore the previous ACL/rule set from the saved config backup.
NGFW.impact: Tightening ACLs can drop production flows — validate against connection logs / flow data in a maintenance window before removing the broad rule.

LOG.risk: Without centralized logging, accurate timestamps, and synchronized time, malicious activity is invisible and incident forensics/attribution are impossible.
steps: Send logs to a central syslog/SIEM collector at an appropriate level.
Enable millisecond timestamps and log the source interface.
Synchronize and authenticate time via NTP.
LOG.cli:
logging host <SIEM-IP>
logging trap informational
logging source-interface <MGMT-IF>
service timestamps log datetime msec show-timezone
service timestamps debug datetime msec
ntp authenticate
ntp server <NTP-IP> key 1
LOG.verify: show logging ; show ntp status ; show run | include service timestamps
LOG.rollback: Remove with 'no logging host <ip>' / 'no ntp server <ip>'.
LOG.impact: Non-disruptive. Ensure the syslog collector can absorb the volume; 'informational' can be chatty on busy devices.

CISCO-CVE.risk: The device is running a software train affected by a published Cisco PSIRT advisory. Depending on the CVE, an attacker may achieve remote code execution, authentication bypass, privilege escalation, or denial of service.
steps: Identify the exact running version (show version).
Consult the referenced Cisco Security Advisory and upgrade to its first-fixed release for your train.
If patching must be deferred, apply the advisory's mitigation and/or disable or ACL-restrict the affected feature (e.g. WebVPN, HTTP server, Smart Install).
CISCO-CVE.gui: Cisco Software Download / DNA Center SWIM for image upgrade; feature toggles per platform manager.
CISCO-CVE.cli:
show version
! example interim mitigations (feature-dependent):
no ip http server
no ip http secure-server
no vstack           ! disable Smart Install
! ASA/FTD: restrict or disable WebVPN per the advisory until patched
CISCO-CVE.verify: show version ; (after upgrade) confirm the build is at or above the advisory's first-fixed release
CISCO-CVE.rollback: Roll back to the previous image from bootflash/disk0 if the upgrade regresses; re-enable a feature only if required and patched.
CISCO-CVE.impact: A software upgrade REBOOTS the device — schedule a maintenance window and ensure HA/redundancy. Disabling WebVPN/HTTP drops those services until the fix is applied.
